mnemonics: Top-Down: Start with the Goal (Sentence) and work Down to Words. Bottom-Up: Start with Words and Build Up to the Sentence.
keyPoints: Top-down parsing starts with the root node (S) and applies grammar rules to expand non-terminals until it matches the input sentence.
Bottom-up parsing begins with the words (terminals) and uses grammar rules in reverse to reduce sequences up to the root node (S).
Top-down is goal-driven (predictive) while bottom-up is data-driven (shift-reduce).
commonMistakes: Assuming both parsing strategies can handle left-recursive grammar equally well (top-down parsers often fail with left-recursion).
Confusing the starting point: trying to build a parse tree from the words up when thinking about a top-down algorithm.
